We introduce OSMO, an open-source wearable tactile glove designed for human-to-robot skill transfer. The glove features 12 three-axis tactile sensors across the fingertips and palm and is designed to be compatible with state-of-the-art hand-tracking methods for in-the-wild data collection. We demonstrate that a robot policy trained exclusively on human demonstrations collected with OSMO, without any real robot data, is capable of executing a challenging contact-rich manipulation task. By equipping both the human and the robot with the same glove, OSMO minimizes the visual and tactile embodiment gap, enabling the transfer of continuous shear and normal force feedback while avoiding the need for image inpainting or other vision-based force inference. On a real-world wiping task requiring sustained contact pressure, our tactile-aware policy achieves a 72% success rate, outperforming vision-only baselines by eliminating contact-related failure modes. We release complete hardware designs, firmware, and assembly instructions to support community adoption.

title: Graphene-Integrated Perforation-Filled Octagon Dielectric Resonator Metasurface for Multifunctional Terahertz Applications
authors: Ankit Chandachoriya, Ranjeet Pratap Singh Bhadoriya, Anil Kumar, Ravi Panwar
abstract: This work introduces a new kind of multifunctional metasurface (MS) structure composed of graphene-integrated perforation-filled octagon dielectric resonator (PFODR) for terahertz (THz) applications. It is noticed that a PFODR-MS absorber exhibits near-unity absorption (99%) at multiple frequencies (3.43, 3.85, 3.96, and 4.42 THz). The narrow absorption peaks indicate high-quality factor (<inline-formula xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink"> <tex-math notation="LaTeX">$Q$</tex-math> </inline-formula> factor) behavior, enabling high sensitivity to refractive index (RI) variations. Using this characteristic, a PFODR-graphene-loop MS-based biosensor is developed, exhibiting high sensitivity and figure of merit (FoM) for detecting changes in the surrounding dielectric environment. The RI-dependent response enables the identification of biological analytes such as the human immunodeficiency virus (HIV), the dengue virus, and glucose solutions. In addition, a graphene octagonal patch-based reconfigurable intelligent surface (RIS) is proposed, achieving ~80% reflection efficiency and 180° phase modulation at 2.96 THz. The results highlight the potential of the proposed structure for multifunctional THz applications, like absorption, biosensing, and wireless communication.

title: Graph Rotation Network: Equivariant Graph Neural Network for Efficient Inverse Design in 4-D Printing_supp1-3679129.pdf
authors: Tao Yue
abstract: Four-dimensional printed structures transform shape under external stimuli, and designing these structures to achieve desired deformation outcomes is crucial for 4-D printing technology. Current machine-learning (ML)-assisted methods can design 4-D-printed structures rapidly and accurately, but often overlook the E(3) equivariance required for the model, leading to inconsistent prediction structure between different coordinate representations of the same structure. Consequently, many ML-assisted design approaches rely on data augmentation techniques such as rotation to improve the model’s generalization across different coordinate representations, but the method through data augmentation is inefficient. We observe that equivariant graph neural networks (GNNs), due to their equivariant properties, can address the inefficiencies associated with the aforementioned training augmentation. Therefore, we propose utilizing an equivariant GNN to improve these efficiencies. Nonetheless, existing equivariant GNNs struggle to balance computational efficiency with expressive power, which will impact their effectiveness in 4-D printing structure design. In response, we introduce a novel equivariant GNN, the graph rotation network (GRN), which approximates arbitrary E(3) equivariant functions by constructing a 3-D equivariant basis through rotations. This approach enhances the model’s performance with trainable rotational angles. We theoretically analyze the E(3) equivariance of our model as well as its computational complexity and empirically demonstrate the improved training efficiency introduced by equivariance. Finally, we apply GRN to both forward prediction and inverse design of 4-D-printed meshes. Our equivariant GNN significantly outperforms nonequivariant GNNs and other equivariant GNNs in forward prediction tasks. For inverse design, our method achieves an accuracy of 98.8% (relative to the mesh size), providing an effective tool for the design of 4-D-printed structures.

title: Predicting Memory Footprint and Selectivity for Query Processing: A Micro Model Approach
authors: Bhargava Rathod
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: This paper proposes a micro-model approach for predicting memory footprint and selectivity in database query processing. We develop and analyze micro models for various instruction types—including arithmetic, limit, aggregate, select, join, and grouping operations—to enhance the simulation and prediction of query execution plans. Our methodology leverages argument size prediction, k-nearest neighbors (kNN) for select operations, and extrapolation for joins. Experiments on TPC-H and air traffic queries reveal that while our method performs well with sufficient training data, challenges persist with complex joins and non-uniform data distributions. The results underscore the need for improved prediction techniques in such scenarios. Future work will focus on enhancing prediction accuracy and addressing these limitations.

title: Dynamic Policy Learning for Legged Robot with Simplified Model Pretraining and Model-Homotopy-Inspired Transfer
authors: Dongyun Kang, Min-Gyu Kim, Tae-Gyu Song, Hajun Kim, Sehoon Ha, Hae-Won Park
abstract: Generating dynamic motions for legged robots remains a challenging problem. While reinforcement learning has achieved notable success in various legged locomotion tasks, producing highly dynamic behaviors often requires extensive reward tuning or high-quality demonstrations. Leveraging reduced-order models can help mitigate these challenges. However, the model discrepancy poses a significant challenge when transferring policies to full-body dynamics environments. In this work, we introduce a continuation-based learning framework that combines simplified model pretraining and model-homotopy-inspired transfer to efficiently generate and refine complex dynamic behaviors. First, we pretrain the policy using a single rigid body model to capture core motion patterns in a simplified environment. Next, we employ a continuation strategy to progressively transfer the policy to the full-body environment, minimizing performance loss. To define the continuation path, we introduce a parametric transition path from the single rigid body model to the full-body model by gradually redistributing mass and inertia between the trunk and legs. The proposed method achieves faster convergence and demonstrates superior stability during the transfer process compared to baseline methods. Our framework is validated on a range of dynamic tasks, including flips and wall-assisted maneuvers, and is successfully deployed on a real quadrupedal robot.

title: Towards Actionable Fashion AI: A Holistic, End-to-End Style Recommendation Assistant
authors: Dhruvi Paprunia, Vansh Kharidia, Samira Deepak, Vijayetha Thoday
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Fashion recommendations often rely on fragmented cloud-based systems that compromise user privacy. We present an end-to-end, on-device fashion assistant combining Korean seasonal color analysis with body shape classification to generate holistic styling guidance. Our system fine-tunes DinoV2 vision transformers achieving 61% seasonal accuracy and employs Qwen3-7B vision-language models achieving 58% body shape accuracy on a new curated celebrity dataset (1,482 images, 4 body types). A rule-based engine integrates color theory with silhouette recommendations, uniquely providing both positive suggestions and negative guidance ("what to avoid"). The system runs entirely on-device with 5-second end-to-end latency on 8GB RAM smartphones, eliminating cloud dependency while delivering 20–25 personalized recommendations per session. Our contributions include a novel body shape dataset, a hybrid vision transformer and vision-language model approach, and complete local processing for privacy-preserving fashion recommendations.

title: Building Trust in Automated Decision-Making: Explainable AI as a Compliance-First Adoption Strategy
authors: Suphawatchara Malanond, Pongsarun Boonyopakorn
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference on Cybernetics and Innovations (ICCI)
abstract: Trust is a recurrent bottleneck in the adoption and diffusion of AI-enabled services, especially where systems perform automated decision-making (ADM) that produces legal or similarly significant effects. This paper advances a compliance-first account of trust for AI adoption: in regulated contexts, users and regulators frequently operationalize trust as demonstrable legal compliance, not merely model accuracy or usability. Drawing on doctrinal analysis of Article 22 GDPR and supervisory guidance on ADM and profiling, and on framework synthesis using the NIST AI Risk Management Framework (AI RMF), we address three research questions: (RQ1) How does legal compliance function as a trust antecedent in AI adoption contexts? (RQ2) What XAI governance controls can be mapped to GDPR Article 22 obligations and AI RMF functions? (RQ3) What compliance gap patterns are observable across documented AI governance failures? Seven publicly documented AI incidents-spanning conversational AI, facial recognition, credit decisioning, public benefits administration, and algorithmic recruitment-are analyzed as boundary cases. The principal contributions are: (i) a compliance-first trust framework connecting legal obligations to Technology Acceptance Model/Diffusion adoption antecedents; (ii) a five-control XAI governance mapping (TABLE II); and (iii) a validated Compliance Gap Scoring Matrix (TABLE III) providing the first structured cross-sector empirical mapping of XAI governance deficits to adoption-relevant trust failures. We propose an implementable set of XAI controls (ex-ante governance, human oversight design, explanation artifacts, contestability workflows, and audit logging) and argue that compliance-aligned explainability provides a measurable pathway to improve adoption outcomes while reducing legal and reputational risk.

title: DV-mBERT: A Reproducible and Transparent Framework for Code-Mixed Bangla Smishing Detection with Meta-Feature Fusion and FGM Robustness
authors: Md Intishar Ahmed, Ahammad Hossen Eazaz, Golam Md. Muradul Bashir
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Smishing (SMS phishing) is escalating globally and is acute in Bangla, where messages are often code-mixed/romanized and lightly obfuscated. We tackle three-way Bangla SMS detection (normal, promo, smish) with a compact multilingual BERT (mBERT) that encodes a dual view of each message—lightly cleaned raw text plus a normalized companion (digit/romanization folding, URL/phone masking)—and fuses simple meta-features (has_latin/link/phone, length, digits). Robustness is enhanced via embedding-level Fast Gradient Method (FGM). To ensure credible measurement, we canonicalize, deduplicate by (canon,label), and use stratified group splits with zero overlap. On a held-out grouped test set ($\mathbf{N} \boldsymbol{=} \mathbf{1 4 6 1}$), mBERT attains Accuracy 0.9979 and MacroF1 0.9980, outperforming TF-IDF+LogReg (0.9802) and XLMRoBERTa (0.9861); under character-level jitter, Macro-F1 remains $0.9945(\Delta \mathbf{F 1}=-0.0035)$. Slice-wise results (Bangla-only, codemixed, has-link, has-phone) confirm consistent gains.

title: Adaptive Message Optimization in WhatsApp Marketing Using Multi-Armed Bandit Algorithms
authors: Priya Trivedi, Sri Gayathri Adabala, Asifa Bandulal Beedi, Yatharth Jain, Aditya Pandey
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: This research explores the design, implementation, and evaluation of an adaptive message optimization framework for WhatsApp marketing using Multi-Armed Bandit (MAB) algorithms. Traditional A/B testing suffers from slow adaptation, rigid traffic splits, and high opportunity costs when low-performing variants still receive equal exposure. To address these limitations, the proposed system applies Thompson Sampling, Upper Confidence Bound (UCB), and Epsilon-Greedy algorithms to dynamically allocate traffic to message variants demonstrating higher engagement. A hypothetical jewelry business is used as an experimental sandbox, with click-through rates (CTR) recorded through uniquely parameterized tracking links integrated with a serverless backend. Results show that Thompson Sampling outperforms static A/B testing under the simulated and pilot study conditions by achieving higher cumulative rewards and faster convergence. This work provides a lightweight, scalable, and data-driven optimization framework suitable for small and medium-sized businesses seeking automated marketing performance improvements.

title: Design and Evaluation of AI-Based Energy-Efficient Load Balancing Algorithm in Cloud Data Centers
authors: Vikas Sharma, Anita Chaudhary, Sonam Kumari, Tarun Kumar Vashishth, Kajal Choudhary, Puneet Chauhan
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The rapid growth in the cloud computing environment has led to an increase in the energy consumption of large-scale data centers. This has led to an increase in costs, efficiency, and sustainability issues. Traditional load balancing algorithms allocate workloads to individual operating resources, but do not account for energy consumption in resource utilization efficiency. We present detailed design and testing of a load balancing algorithm based on artificial intelligence for the energy efficient operation of cloud data centers. In contrast, it realizes its efficiency by the availability of real time decision making and adaptive workload consolidation by usage of the algorithm in assigning the workloads to the resources reducing the idle server time and optimally power utilization, without reducing system efficiency or violating service level agreements (SLAs). The proposed model uses a machine-learning-based workload prediction mechanism for dynamic resource allocation. The AI-EELBA model was simulated in CloudSim. In terms of energy consumption, SLA compliance and response time it proved to be 28% lower, 61% more compliant, and 27% faster than the Round Robin and Throttled algorithms, respectively. The results indicate the model's potentials for sustainable high performance cloud operations.

title: Intelligent Selection System for Bad Soil Geological Treatment Solutions Based on Deep Learning
authors: Zhongliang Cao, Haibo Meng, Hongyu Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: As infrastructure construction expands into geologically complex regions, the treatment of unfavorable soil geology (such as soft soil, karst, landslides, high liquid limit soil, and expansive soil) has become a critical aspect of engineering construction. The selection of appropriate treatment solutions directly impacts project safety, cost, and schedule. Current solution selection often relies heavily on engineers' experience, which is subject to subjectivity, inefficiency, and susceptibility to regional and experience differences, making it difficult to adapt to complex and changing geological conditions. First, a database of unfavorable soil geological characteristics and treatment solutions is constructed, integrating representative domestic and international engineering cases (including the G80 Guangkun Expressway Nanning to Baise section expansion and reconstruction project, which addressed high liquid limit soil and expansive soil) with geological survey data. Second, a hybrid model combining CNN (convolutional neural network) and LSTM (long short-term memory network) is designed based on deep learning technology to deeply extract geological characteristics and model the matching relationship between solutions. Finally, an intelligent selection system is developed, integrating data preprocessing, model inference, solution recommendation, and result visualization. Experiments show that the standard deviation of sensitivity tests for all parameters is less than 3%, and the system demonstrates good adaptability in complex scenarios with multiple geological factors.

title: LGDTI-Net A Lesion-Aware Dynamic Transformer Framework for Precise Small Kidney Lesion Segmentation in Multi-Centre Medical Imaging
authors: A.R. Kavitha, P. Sathiyaseelan
publish: 2026 International Conference on Data Science, Agents and Artificial Intelligence (ICDSAAI)
abstract: Small kidney lesions are still very challenging to detect in clinical imaging because of small datasets that have few annotations, subtle visual features, and inter-scanner variability. The fact that these lesions can be very low-contrast and smoky-bordered can also make it difficult to define them across different imaging conditions precisely. Although they are effective at detecting local spatial patterns, traditional convolutional networks can blur fine-grained structural information during the extraction of deep features. To overcome these limitations, this study uses transformer-based models from across the globe to attenuate subtle pathological indicators. It introduces LGDTI-Net, a lesion-aware dynamic transformer framework designed to reliably segment small kidney lesions in multi-centre CT and ultrasound datasets. The architecture is based on multi-scale convolutional feature extraction and a lesion-proposal attention mechanism that selectively activates transformer reasoning on suspicious areas. Experimental evaluation demonstrates that better features are added to a structured decoder to achieve accurate boundary delineation and, overall, improved stability of generalisation and clinically interpretable probability mapping.

title: Fusion of EfficientNetB0 and ResNet50 for Enhanced Deepfake Video Detection
authors: Deepak Sharma, Gaurav Garg, Jasneet Chawla
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Deepfake generation raises major concerns regarding digital security, media integrity, and identity protection in the cyberspace, especially since it allows producing extraordinarily believable fake videos via artificial intelligence and deep learning methods. The existing methods of deepfake detection are focused on the identification of visual abnormalities such as face distortions, unusual smoothness in face area, irregularities at edges, and changes in illumination. In order to enhance accuracy of deepfake video detection, the paper introduces a hybrid deep learning model that integrates ResNet50 for learn deep and robust representations, EfficientNetB0 to make sure efficient feature extraction and a custom data pre-processing pipeline. To train and evaluate the model, the UADFV dataset was used, in which the extracted features of the two state of the art models are fused together and then passed through fully connected layers with softmax activation in order to classify binary data. The proposed system has a accuracy of 93.89% which is very effective in the differentiation of real and manipulated video content. The research enhances the deepfake detection techniques and contributes to the research of media forensics to help reduce the spread of the digital misinformation.

title: A Dual-Channel Entity Relation Extraction Framework with Relation Perception
authors: Zheng Ren, Ling Lu, Bei Xiang, Yirui Fan
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: Entity relation triple extraction aims to identify subjects, objects, and their relations from text. Existing relation-prior methods alleviate overlapping triples but still struggle with: (1) increased complexity from one-to-many mappings, and (2) early relation decisions made before sufficient semantic coupling, which can cause irreversible errors. To address this, we propose a dual-channel extraction framework with relational context awareness. A Transformer-based non-autoregressive decoder first captures potential relational context as semantic guidance for entity detection. Then, a complementary dual-channel strategy—R2S (Relation to Head to Tail) and R2O (Relation to Tail to Head)—generates entity pairs in parallel from different semantic perspectives, reducing omissions in single-path decoding. Additionally, a relation-guided feature fusion module based on BiLSTM integrates relational context with entity representations to enhance boundary detection and feature discrimination. Experiments on multiple public datasets demonstrate that our model achieves strong performance and robustness, especially in one-to-many and complex sentence scenarios, validating the effectiveness of relation-guided dual-channel modeling.

title: Deep Learning for Knee Osteoarthritis Detection and Severity Grading: A Comprehensive Study Using Radiographic Imaging and Grad-CAM Interpretability
authors: Vinothkumar Kolluru, Naveen L, Akshatha B S, Shreyas Rajendra Hole, Thirunaavukkarasu Murugesan, Ajeeb Sagar
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Knee Osteoarthritis (OA), like other forms of degenerative arthrosis, is characterised by progressive joint degeneration, the formation of bone spurs in the sub-chondral bone, and narrowing of the joint spaces and is one of the most disabling conditions worldwide. For the OA diagnosis, radiographic imaging is the most used and best standard method, and the Kellgren–Lawrence (KL) grading system is the most commonly used to assess severity. Unfortunately, manual grading takes a lot of time and is subject to inter-observer variability. For this gap, we created a deep learning supervised model for automated OA detection and severity classification, using the publicly available Knee Osteoarthritis Dataset with Severity Grading. For this, we trained several and varied architectures of convolutional neural networks (CNN), including VGG16 and MobileNet. For the model created, we used contrast normalisation, histogram equalisation, and image resizing to 224x224 pixels, followed with extensive data augmentation. The models created in this research are trained using transfer learning with weights from ImageNet, and the Adam optimiser with a categorical cross entropy loss. Moreover, for joint space narrowing and bone spurs, we used Grad-CAM for attention and alignment of the clinically relevant features to explain the decision of the model. Based on experimental outcomes, MobileNet exhibits the greatest balance between precision and efficiency, attaining an overall classification precision of 87.3%. With respect to the VGG16 and baseline models, the weighted F1 score and Cohen’s kappa were 0.85 and 0.81, respectively, signifying that MobileNet is the most accurate and reliable. Grad-CAM visualisations depict the integrating areas which correspond to the radiological features of OA, which increases the clinical trustworthiness. This deep learning study highlights the precision, interpretability and scalability available for the diagnosis and grading of the clinical and radiological features of knee OA, emphasising the technology’s potential to aid large-scale screening and clinical decision-making.

title: Attention-Enhanced EfficientNetB3 for Diabetic Retinopathy Classification using Fundus Image
authors: Ravi Bhushan Dixit, Chandan Kumar Jha
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Diabetic retinopathy is a serious complication of the eye caused by prolonged diabetes. It may result in complete blindness if not detected at an early stage. Manual detection of DR using a fundus image is a tedious and error-prone task. In past, various deep-learning (DL) based techniques have been developed to assist doctors in the detection and severity grading of DR. However, these techniques require further improvement in classification performance and complexity. In this paper, an EfficientNetB3 with Squeeze and Excitation (SE) block attention module is proposed for DR detection. EfficientNetB3 extracts features of the fundus image using a scaling mechanism. Further SE block attached to the final layer gives more attention to extracted features by highlighting relevant regions of the retina, such as microaneurysms, haemorrhages, and exudates. This approach uses contrast-limited adaptive histogram equalisation (CLAHE), advanced augmentation techniques, and class weighting to improve the quality of the image and to handle the class imbalance and overfitting problem. Moreover, GradCaM, T-SNE, and uniform manifold approximation and projection (UMAP) visualization techniques are used for class separability and interpretability of the model. The proposed technique is experimented with using fundus images of the APTOS-2019 dataset. It is also evaluated on the IDRiD and Messidor-2 datasets for model generalizability. The proposed model achieves an accuracy of 98.91%, precision of 98.91%, sensitivity of 98.91%, specificity of 98.90%, F1 score of 98.91%, kappa score of 97.80% using the APTOS-2019 dataset. It is found that the proposed technique outperforms many DL based techniques used for diabetic retinopathy classification.

title: A Cumulative Exposure Assessment Method for Low-Frequency Magnetic Field for Electric Vehicles Based on Peak Detection
authors: Siyang Zhao, Ruohao Wang, Jiale Fu, Xing Cui, Peifeng Cui, Haijun She
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: This paper investigates the assessment method for low-frequency magnetic filed based on peak detection for electric vehicles. When applying the GB 8702 criterion to 20 actual EV measurements using full-frequency-point accumulation, the compliance rate was 0%, indicating overly conservative exposure assessment. This paper establishes an assessment model comprising threshold screening and peak detection. This approach elevates the compliance rate and yields a logically physical interpretation. Furthermore, it conducts an in-depth analysis of the significance of varying thresholds and peak detection, thereby providing a more scientifically grounded engineering methodology for the electromagnetic field safety assessment of electric vehicles.

title: Multi-Agent Collaborative Scheduling and Emission Reduction Mechanism for Minimizing the Carbon Footprint of Regional Electricity-Heat-Gas Coupled Systems
authors: Rui Chen
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The heterogeneous nature of multiple energy sources and decentralized decision-making among multiple agents in a regional electricity-heat-gas coupled system make it difficult to collaboratively optimize the system's total carbon emissions. This study proposes a collaborative emission reduction mechanism that integrates carbon-aware multi-agent collaborative scheduling with cooperative game-based emission reduction cost sharing. Methodologically, a multi-energy flow physical model characterizing the dynamic characteristics and coupling units of the electricity, heat, and gas networks is constructed, and a carbon flow tracking mechanism is introduced to quantify the carbon footprint of the entire chain. A multi-agent proximal policy optimization (MAPPO) algorithm is then used for distributed collaborative scheduling optimization. Finally, emission reduction costs and benefits are fairly shared based on Shapley values. The proposed collaborative scheduling strategy effectively reduces system carbon emissions, reducing total carbon emissions by 14.6% (from 1432.6 tons to 1223.3 tons) over the entire period, while also saving 12.1% in operating costs. This study provides a technical solution and incentive mechanism for the low-carbon, economical, and stable operation of regional integrated energy systems.

title: Real-Time Implementation of ANFIS-Backstepping MPPT for PV-SEPIC Converters
authors: Khalil Chnini, Dalinda Dhifaoui, Houda Jouini, Hatem Allagui
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: The nonlinear relationship between voltage and current in photovoltaic (PV) systems requires advanced control strategies to ensure maximum power extraction under dynamic environmental conditions. This paper proposes a hybrid Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) approach combining an Adaptive Neuro-Fuzzy Inference System (ANFIS) with Backstepping control for PV systems using a SEPIC converter. The ANFIS module estimates the optimal reference voltage, while the Backstepping controller guarantees fast and stable duty-cycle regulation. The proposed method is implemented through a Processor-in-the-Loop (PIL) setup on an ARM-based STM32F407VG microcontroller using a Model-Based Design (MBD) workflow, ensuring real-time feasibility. Simulation and experimental results demonstrate 99.6% tracking efficiency with a 9 ms response time, outperforming ANN-BS and classical P&O methods. These results confirm the proposed controller's robustness and its suitability for low-cost embedded PV applications.

title: Hybrid Motion Synthesis for Speech-to-Sign Translation Using Deterministic Linguistic Parsing and Gated Recurrent Units
authors: B. Priyanka, G. Uday Kiran, Srinivasarao Sanapala, Sreevedh Jella, Velamarthi Karthik, Vontela Gopika
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Sign language is a major means of communication for millions of deaf and hard-of-hearing people all over the world. However, generating grammatically well-structured and expressive sign language animation remains an open challenge due to the complex interplay between linguistic structure and human motion. A hybrid framework is proposed for speech-to-sign translation, which incorporates deterministic linguistic parsing with neural motion modeling. First, structured gloss representations from spoken language input are generated by a rule-constrained language model. Then, motion synthesis is carried out by a hybrid mechanism that combines Gated Recurrent Units with geometric interpolation for smooth, biologically plausible transitions between signs. The proposed system operates on pre-extracted skeletal motion data and allows modular extension for different sign languages. Experiments show improved temporal smoothness and interpretability compared to purely interpolative baselines. The framework provides a scalable, language-agnostic approach for sign language generation while preserving transparency and controllability.

title: A Self-Powering-First Power Management Scheme for Energy Harvesting Systems with Zero Standby Power Consumption
authors: Dejian Li, Zhihua Wang, Yan Ma, Zhe Wang, Shuaishuai Wang
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: This paper presents a self-powering-first power management scheme and a low voltage cold-start circuit for thermoelectric energy harvesting system. The harvester operates by first channeling the harvested ambient energy to a dedicated capacitor that powers itself. Only the surplus energy, beyond what the harvester consumes, is then directed to the battery. This self-powering-first design ensures that the harvester runs entirely on ambient energy while storing the excess. When ambient energy is unavailable, this mechanism prevents the harvester from draining the battery. Once the ambient energy returns, the cold-start circuit reactivates the system to resume operation. The proposed energy harvester is designed using a <tex xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">$0.18 \mu \mathrm{m}$</tex> CMOS process. Post-simulation results indicate a maximum harvesting efficiency of 86.5% and a minimum cold-start voltage as low as 96 mV.

title: AI-Powered Forecasting Models, Optimizing Working Capital, Supply Chain Financing
authors: Sreenivasulu Gajula
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The ever-growing nature of modern enterprise ecosystems has exacerbated the necessity of smart/intelligent systems that can predict financial needs, smart/intelligent liquidity deployment and risk hedging in volatile markets. This paper presents a new dynamic adaptive model, Dynamic Adaptive Neural Capital Structuring and Transaction Optimization Framework (DynaCast-X), that uses artificial intelligence to optimise management of financial flows and enterprise transactions. The framework combines time graph learning, deep reinforcement optimization and probabilistic risk modelling to deliver a dynamic and robust decision-making framework in the financial sector. The temporal graph learning module models enterprise exchanges and supplier-buyer connections as dynamically evolving graphs, and this allows both recording the immediate changes and the long-term changes in the financial cycles. The reinforcement-based optimization algorithm uses deep reinforcement learning to optimize liquidity reallocation activities in an efficient manner with ensuring minimal reserves bound in vain in order to meet financial stability. Moreover, the hybrid risk-sensitivity layer is integrated with Bayesian network and adversarial stress testing in order to forecast systemic shocks like credit volatility or delayed receivables. The DynaCast-X goes beyond static-based forecasting, and classic financial models that rely on simplicity and lack of complexity as their most prominent features. The framework provides insights to understand how it can be used to alter the financial landscape by helping build agility, resilience as well as sustainable value creation in enterprise functions. This study extends the theoretical knowledge and reference with actual experience on the implementation of the next-generation AI-driven financial optimization systems. The suggested technique attains an overall accuracy of 97.4 percent, illustrating its exceptional forecasting capacity.

title: A Wavelet-Statistical Method for EEG Signal Classification of Mental Stress in Cognitive Tasks
authors: Md Obaydullah Khan, Jahid Hassan, Md Robiul Islam, Nazmul Haque Anik, Syed Udoy Ahmed, Md Al Emran
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Real-time electroencephalogram (EEG) workload indicators can significantly enhance complex task performance when integrated into man-machine interfaces. This study focuses on electroencephalograms (EEGs) and their application in stress detection. Stress is an inevitable aspect of human life, and analyzing stress is even more crucial than analyzing sleep in many contexts. EEG provides a reliable method for monitoring the brain’s electrical activity. In this study, features were extracted from a single-channel EEG signal recorded both before and during the mental task. These features were computed in the time-frequency domain using wavelet decomposition, providing valuable insights into stress-related brain dynamics. The decomposed EEG signals exhibited significant variations in amplitude, power, and frequency during mental stress compared to the pre-arithmetic task condition. From each decomposed signal, twelve advanced statistical features were extracted within a defined frame, resulting in a total of 60 features across five frequency bands. After feature selection, four key characteristics were identified and used for classification with the Ensemble Subspace KNN classifier. When evaluated on data from 11 participants using a five-fold cross-validation procedure, the classifier achieved an accuracy of 78.1%, outperforming several state-of-the-art methods reported in the literature.

title: BanglaMM-Disaster: A Multimodal Transformer-Based Deep Learning Framework for Multiclass Disaster Classification in Bangla
authors: Ariful Islam, Md Rifat Hossen, Md. Mahmudul Arif, Abdullah Al Noman, Md Arifur Rahman
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Natural disasters remain a major challenge for Bangladesh, so real-time monitoring and quick response systems are essential. In this study, we present BanglaMM-Disaster, an end-to-end deep learning-based multimodal framework for disaster classification in Bangla, using both textual and visual data from social media. We constructed a new dataset of $\mathbf{5, 0 3 7}$ Bangla social media posts, each consisting of a caption and a corresponding image, annotated into one of nine disasterrelated categories. The proposed model integrates transformerbased text encoders, including BanglaBERT, mBERT, and XLMRoBERTa, with CNN backbones such as ResNet50, DenseNet169, and MobileNetV2, to process the two modalities. Using early fusion, the best model achieves 83.76% accuracy. This surpasses the best text-only baseline by 3.84% and the image-only baseline by 16.91%. Our analysis also shows reduced misclassification across all classes, with noticeable improvements for ambiguous examples. This work fills a key gap in Bangla multimodal disaster analysis and demonstrates the benefits of combining multiple data types for real-time disaster response in low-resource settings.

title: Dynamic 5G Bandwidth Allocation in SDN Using Round Robin Heuristic: A Quality-of-Service Optimization Approach
authors: Mark D’souza, Parikshith Patil, Shreya Das, Attahar Faizan, Anand Meti
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: This paper outlines a mechanism for dynamic 5G bandwidth allocation in Software-Defined Networks (SDN) based on a Round Robin heuristic to enhance Quality of Service (QoS). The massive growth in mobile data and bandwidth-consuming applications require effective resource handling in future wireless networks such as 5G. Conventional network structures face challenges in the dynamic requirements of various 5G services. SDN provides a solution through decoupling the control and data planes to facilitate centralized, programmable traffic management. Current SDN bandwidth mechanisms tend to suffer from flow starvation and unfair allocation. To mitigate these, this work suggests a customized Round Robin algorithm deployed on a Mininet-based 5G topology controlled by a custom Ryu controller. The solution uses weighted proportional allocation, dynamic time slicing according to service priorities, and controller load monitoring. Performance indicators such as throughput, latency, and fairness are measured and compared with traditional approaches. The experimental environment, controller architecture, scheduling logic, and software platform are explained, including the simulation framework developed on Mininet 2.3.0, Ryu 4.34+, and Python 3.8+ on Ubuntu 20.04 LTS, and analysis using Excel/Pandas. This heuristic is intended to offer a light but efficient solution for equitable and efficient bandwidth allocation in heterogeneous 5G networks.

title: Hybrid Residual CNN with Convolutional Attention for Kannada Text Recognition
authors: Mahantesh S Hiremath, Hrushikesh P Karamadi, Abrar Ali Savadatti, Satish Chikkamath, Kaushik M B, Suneeta V. Budihal
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Handwritten Kannada text recognition is challenging due to the script’s curved structure, compound characters(vattakshara), and variations in writing style. Most existing OCR systems focus on character-level recognition and struggle with full words, especially when strokes overlap or ligatures appear. This work presents a segmentation-free OCR system for handwritten Kannada words using a hybrid architecture that combines a ResNet-CBAM backbone, Bidirectional LSTMs, multi-head self attention, and CTC decoding. The CNN extracts spatial features using the channel and spatial attention, after which the representation is projected into a positional sequence. BiLSTMs capture local character dependencies, and multi-head attention refines long-range interactions across the word. The model is trained end-to-end without handcrafted features or alignment, Evaluated on the IIIT-Indic Kannada handwritten word dataset, the system achieves 91.17% character accuracy and 79.87% word accuracy, outperforming multiple CNN, CRNN, and Transformer baselines. The results highlight the value of combining attention-enhanced features with sequence modelling for Indic OCR.

title: Comparative Performance Analysis of Dual-Band GPS Phased Array Configurations with MVDR Beamforming
authors: Md Shaikat Hossain, Md Aminul Islam
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Global Positioning System (GPS) receivers remain highly vulnerable to intentional interference such as jamming and spoofing, threatening navigation reliability in both civilian and military applications. Recent research has demonstrated the effectiveness of dual-band operation and adaptive beamforming as independent mitigation strategies. However, limited attention has been given to the comparative evaluation of phased array configurations operating under dualband conditions with adaptive algorithms such as Minimum Variance Distortionless Response (MVDR). This paper presents a comparative study of three Uniform Rectangular Array (URA) sizes- $8 \times 8$, $11 \times 11$, and $16 \times 16$-integrated with MVDR beamforming for spoofing mitigation. Simulation results conducted in MATLAB using the Phased Array System Toolbox confirm that the $11 \times 11$ URA achieves the best balance between accuracy, complexity, and scalability, with null depths of - $\mathbf{3 4. 5}$ dB, an SNR improvement of 16.8 dB, and latency of 0.075 seconds. Comparative benchmarking against recent IEEE studies confirms that the proposed configuration provides 5-7 $\mathbf{d B}$ deeper nulls and faster response than existing anti-spoofing arrays. While the $16 \times 16$ URA achieves marginally better nulling, the increased computational burden outweighs its benefits, whereas the $8 \times 8$ URA offers cost savings but sacrifices robustness. These findings highlight the optimal trade-off in phased array design for dual-band GPS receivers and provide new insights into scalable anti-spoofing solutions.

title: Medical IoT Authentication and Security Through Three Point Authentication and Access Token
authors: Piyush Kumar Thakur, Harshit Rajesh Benke, Harshita Shankar, Priyanka Biswas, Nirmalya Kar
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Recently, a shift in biomedical technology has occurred, specifically towards remote patient monitoring. This however, also increases the chances of cyberattacks like sensitive data theft, impersonation because of which many of systems face issues such as DDoS attacks, impersonation, MITM. Existing security mechanisms typically address these threats in isolation, focusing on either traffic filtering, authentication, or data-integrity protection. The proposed model aims to benefit the aforementioned networks by ensuring their safety as well as efficiency, using a comprehensive three-point system, where the three points are namely an authorization server, nodes and a database/middleware, each securely communicating with each other independent of the third point that ensures authenticity, efficiency, and resilience against the attack. The following model is has primary objective of minimizing the computational and communication costs while delivering the optimal efficiency with reduced overhead in authentication process and higher read-write efficiency under strict time and hardware constraints.

title: Hybrid Transformer-Detection-Prompt Pipeline for HighPrecision Segmentation of Renal Microvasculature in Histopathology Imaging
authors: Sakhawat Hossen, Imrul Hasan Rahat, M Waqif Ul Huq, Md. Arafath Yeasin Nihan, Md. Abdullah Sumon
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Accurately mapping the renal microvasculature from histopathology slides is an important step in identifying and monitoring chronic kidney diseases. Conventional CNNbased segmentation models such as U-Net or DeepLab often fail to capture the fine, elongated vessels in detail, resulting in broken or incomplete masks. To address these shortcomings, we developed a three-stage hybrid framework that combines object detection, transformer-based segmentation, and promptdriven refinement. In the first stage, YOLOv8 rapidly identifies candidate vascular regions. These localized patches are then segmented with Swin-Unet, which benefits from transformer-based global context to maintain vessel continuity. Finally, the Segment Anything Model (SAM) is used to refine boundaries, producing cleaner and more anatomically consistent masks. The system also applies morphological post-processing and ensemble fusion to further improve connectivity and noise removal. Experiments on the HuBMAP kidney vasculature dataset show that the proposed pipeline achieves a Dice score of 0.8927 and an IoU of 0.8421, outperforming all single-model baselines. Qualitative results confirm better edge definition, smoother continuity, and improved preservation of thin capillaries. The modular design allows easy adaptation to other biomedical segmentation tasks and can be deployed in real clinical workflows with modest computing resources.

title: Leveraging Vision Transformer to Classify Cancer Stages from Breast Ultrasound Images
authors: Muhammad Junayed, Tanzeem Tahmeed Reza
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Breast cancer occurs when breast cells mutate and form tumors. While ultrasound imaging is a valuable tool for detecting and evaluating breast masses, its effectiveness is limited by operator dependency and a restricted field of view. In recent years, deep learning methods have been employed to address these limitations. Among them, Vision Transformer (ViT) models have emerged as a powerful approach for image classification, offering accurate and efficient results. This study leverages ViT to enhance breast cancer detection, ensuring reliable outcomes with reduced time requirements. In this paper, we presented a tailored Vision Transformer-based encoder model to identify and effectively classify the benign, malignant, and normal classes of images from the Breast Ultrasound Images (BUSI) dataset. The proposed approach was compared to an actual vision transformer model, and it was found that our customized model performs better in every way. In our approach, we addressed class imbalance, ensuring fair evaluation across all classes in the BUSI dataset. The results are measured with the performance metrics: accuracy, precision, recall, and f1_score accordingly. The accuracy of the algorithm was 96.98%, with 97.96% precision, 95.46% recall, and f1-score of 96.71% respectively. This proposed method would be much more time-efficient as well as better than other classification algorithms for breast cancer prediction.

title: Enhancing Forest Fire Prediction Through Explainable Ai-Driven Feature Selection: A Case Study in Khenchela Province, Algeria
authors: Abdelhakim Sahour, Mohamed Lamri, Farouk Boumehrez, Abdelaali Bekhouche, Feriel Aouissi, Fouzia Maamri
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: Forest fires increasingly threaten Mediterranean ecosystems, causing substantial ecological and economic damage. This study uses explainable artificial intelligence (XAI) to identify optimal feature subsets for wildfire prediction. We compare three feature selection approaches SHAP values, model-based feature importance, and BorutaSHAP across seven classifiers. To address class imbalance (28.4 % fire events), we applied SMOTE with stratified tenfold cross-validation. Reducing features from 56 to 18 maintains accuracy above 98 % while cutting storage by 67.8 % and training time by 52.3 %. Matthews Correlation Coefficients (MCC) for top models range from 92.89 % to 94.03 %, confirming robust performance. External validation on the UCI Algerian Forest Fires dataset yields comparable results (MCC = 0.89), establishing generalizability. Key predictors consistently identified include historical fire data, fire radiative power, temperature, humidity, and wind speed.

title: Human-Digital Twin Symbiosis Using Cognitive AI for Smart Manufacturing
authors: Raghu Praneeth Akula, Rohitha Gajam, Vinay Kumar Gali
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: With the convergence of Industry 5.0, cyber-physical systems and cognitive artificial intelligence (AI) the Human-Digital Twin (HDT) symbiosis has become the new paradigm of smart manufacturing. As opposed to traditional digital twins, which mostly model physical assets, Human-Digital Twins combine real-time physiological, behavioural and cognitive data of human operators with digital models of machines and processes, allowing humans and autonomous systems to intelligently interact. In this paper, a novel Cognitive AI-based Human-Digital Twin Symbiosis is suggested that uses multimodal sensing, edge-cloud computing, and deep learning models to monitor constantly human performance, contextual awareness, and operational states in the manufacturing environment. In the offered system, there are cognitive reasoning and adaptive decision-making alongside the use of predictive analytics, which contribute to enhancing the humanmachine interaction, maximizing operational efficiency, and achieving safety and reliability. The proposed framework provides an opportunity to apply transformer-based cognitive models and reinforcement learning mechanisms to provide personalized support, workload optimization, and real-time detection of anomalies. Experimental testing indicates that there are vast improvements in production efficiency, operator safety, predictive maintenance accuracy, and system responsiveness with the conventional digital twin architectures. The findings indicate the promise of Cognitive AI-based Human-Digital Twin symbiosis in supporting intelligent, human-friendly manufacturing systems, and this will enhance the vision of resilient, adaptive and collaborative smart factories in the next generation industrial ecosystems.

title: Electric Vehicle-Based Frequency Regulation: A Sustainable Approach for the Power Grid
authors: Jihene Jmal, Mouna Rekik, Lotfi Krichen
publish: 2026 IEEE International conference on Advanced Systems and Emergent Technologies (IC_ASET)
abstract: The integration of renewable energy sources into the power grid has introduced new challenges for frequency stability due to intermittent generation. A promising solution is the use of electric vehicles (EVs) at recharge stations to support grid operations. EVs, with large battery capacities and growing presence at recharge points, can act as mobile energy storage units, charging power when frequency is high and discharging energy when frequency is low. This paper proposes a V2G-based frequency regulation strategy that leverages real-time grid data and the availability of EVs at recharge stations. The method employs multi-threshold frequency detection and adaptive EV power control to actively balance supply and demand. Unlike existing approaches, the strategy integrates recharge station scheduling with coordinated EV participation, enabling more effective frequency support. A detailed EVs fleet model is implemented and tested in MATLAB/Simulink. Simulation results demonstrate that coordinated EVs at recharge stations can significantly reduce frequency deviations and enhance grid stability.

title: A Vision-based System for Monitoring Kitchen Hygiene
authors: Druvya Sravani, Yashwanth Surigi, Sahil Verma, Kranthi Mottu
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Food safety is crucial in the food industry, as maintaining it is vital for preserving a food manufacturer’s reputation and profitability. Nevertheless, hygiene violations by food handlers are one of the primary causes of food quality degradation and foodborne illnesses. The current food safety measures depend heavily on manual inspections and human vigilance to ensure adherence to safety protocols. While these measures are crucial, they are susceptible to human error and lack real-time monitoring capabilities. To this end, the current work proposes a computer vision-based automated system for detecting hairnets and gloves to ensure strict compliance with hygiene regulations in food processing facilities. Additionally, this research fine-tunes three different pre-trained models, namely YOLO (You Only Look Once), Faster R-CNN and Florence-2, using transfer learning on real-time data collected from fifteen different food facilities. Extensive comparative experiments reveal that the YOLOv8m model trained with adamW optimizer obtained the highest mAP50 score of 0.957, demonstrating the system's effectiveness in real-world scenarios.

title: Real-Time Adaptive Self-Calibration of Automotive MIMO Radars Using Machine Learning
authors: Rakesh Yadav Kodari, D. Nagaraju, S. Khadarvali
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Automotive multiple-input–multiple-output (MIMO) radars employing digital beamforming (DBF) are widely used in advanced driver assistance systems (ADAS) due to their robustness under adverse weather and lighting conditions. However, hardware non-idealities such as amplitude and phase mismatches among antenna channels degrade angular accuracy, increase sidelobe levels, and may generate ghost targets. Conventional self-calibration techniques rely on offline procedures or angle-dependent measurements, which are not suitable for real-time adaptation under temperature variations, aging, and dynamic operating conditionsIn this paper, a real-time adaptive self-calibration framework for automotive MIMO radars is proposed using a lightweight machine learning (ML) approach. The method combines physics-based error modeling with data-driven learning to continuously estimate and compensate channel-wise amplitude and phase errors during radar operation. Monte-Carlo simulations are first employed to generate statistically representative training and validation datasets under varying error conditions. The trained ML model predicts correction coefficients from observable radar features such as channel amplitudes, relative phases, and estimated angles of arrival. The proposed approach is validated using measurements from a 77–81 GHz automotive radar platformCompared to conventional self-calibration methods, the proposed ML-based adaptive calibration significantly reduces mainlobe misalignment and sidelobe levels while enabling online correction without explicit angle-dependent calibration procedures. The results demonstrate the feasibility of real-time, angle-independent calibration for next-generation automotive radar systems.

title: Ayurvedic Herb Identification and Classification App using Machine Vision
authors: S R M Rakesh Shankar, Pramod Mathew Jacob
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: Ayurvedic herbs are widely used in medicine and cosmetic products manufacturing. But identifying and recognizing these ayurvedic herbs is a tedious task for the humans as it requires lot of practical knowledege and experience in that field. So, we develop an Ayurvedic herb identification and classification App that will identify medicinal plants through image processing and deep learning processes. The system enables users to submit plant pictures on a web interface, and the images are processed by the trained Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) model. The application not only recognize the herb but also give comprehensive details about the Ayurvedic benefits of the identified herb. The app also has a feature to find nearby shops or sources to buy products related to that herb. The suggested solution is conducted with the use of Python, OpenCV, and deep learning models, including ResNet18 and EfficientNetB0, and further integrates Google Gemini and SerpAPI to generate better responses. We have integrated ancient Ayurvedic knowledge and the modern AI technologies in order to make herbal medicine more accessible and aware among the users. The experimental findings shows that ayurvedic herbs are identified and classified with an accuracy of 97.77% . Thus, our model can be utilized in ayurvedic pharmaceuticals and also among local people.

title: Cavum Septum Pellucidum Instance Segmentation By Brain Point Detection
authors: Shudipta Banik, Israt Jahan, Md. Samiul Islam, Saikat Sarkar, Kamrul Islam, Nafiuzzaman Udoy, Pronobesh Barua
publish: 2025 IEEE International Conference on Signal Processing, Information, Communication and Systems (SPICSCON)
abstract: Cavum Septum Pellucidum (CSP) is a critical brain structure frequently evaluated in neurological and psychiatric assessments which requires precise identification and segmentation for accurate diagnosis and evaluation. This paper introduces the YOLO (You Only Look Once) model within an improved framework of advanced methods for data augmentation. It incorporates an optimized YOLOv9c model which is specifically designed for high-resolution medical imaging that helps to identify key points and outline the boundaries of the CSP. This research paper use four different YOLO version algorithms: YOLOv8m, YOLOv8n, YOLOv8x, and YOLOv9c. Among the models, the YOLOv9c demonstrates significant enhancements in segmentation accuracy attaining high precision $(0.962)$, recall $(0.946)$, and mean average precision (0.980). We evaluate the performance of our model using a comprehensive dataset of brain MRI (Magnetic Resonance Imaging) and it shows robustness and adaptability across a wide range of imaging conditions. In this study, observations indicate that integrating point detection into instance segmentation tasks enhances localization accuracy and optimizes the segmentation process, which makes it highly suitable for real-time clinical applications. This research highlights the promise of integrating state-of-the-art CNN (Convolutional Neural Network) architectures with innovative detection techniques to advance the analysis of medical images, especially within neuroimaging.

title: Comparative Study of Power Quality Metrics in Different Loads across V2G and G2V Scenarios
authors: Jitender Kaushal, Surbhi Gupta, Rahul, Akarshit Barwal
publish: 2025 IEEE 1st International Conference on Recent Trends in Computing and Smart Mobility (RCSM)
abstract: The emergence of Electric Vehicles (EVs) means the establishment of bidirectional power flow which is possible through the operation of V2G and G2V in today's smart grids. In this regard, the document discusses an interactive investigation of the performance of power quality metrics in inductive loads under V2G and G2V scenarios in terms of their effect on grid stability. A comprehensive model of dual flow is developed in MATLAB/Simulink to represent the connection of inductive loads. Total Harmonic Distortion (THD), power factor, and voltage waveforms were determining key parameters of power quality. The results indicate that inductive loads introduce energy quality degradation, mainly associated with a decrease of the power factor and slightly distortion of voltage and current waveforms. However, the system kept permitted limits defined by IEEE 519 and effects were mitigated through converter control strategy. This shows the importance of intelligent power electronics in maintaining performance in the grid and lays the groundwork for future work involving real-time proof and application of sophisticated filtering and compensation methods for a robust EV-grid connection.

title: Efficient Multi Task Molecular Toxicity Prediction Using 2D Graph Isomorphism Networks with Virtual Node Augmentation and Stratified Learning
authors: Mandadi Varun Prakash, Kalluru Sai Dheeraj Reddy, Pramod P, Satish Chikkamath
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Molecular toxicity prediction is an important problem in drug discovery, where models must identify unsafe compounds early to avoid costly late-stage failures. Learning across the 12 Tox21 assays becomes harder due to severe class imbalance, missing labels, and diverse toxicological mechanisms. In this work, a multi-task Graph Isomorphism Network (GIN) approach was used to train on 2D molecular graphs generated from canonical SMILES strings using RDKit. The environment incorporated a learnable virtual node, residual connections, and edge dropout regularization, with multi-label iterative stratification to preserve label distributions across train/validation/test splits. A class-weighted BCE-with-logits loss with label smoothing was applied only to observed labels, using AdamW optimization, gradient clipping, and OneCycleLR scheduling over 150 epochs. Training results showed steady improvement from 0.725 AUC (epoch 1) to 0.955 peak validation AUC, with stable convergence across all endpoints. Visualization of per-task thresholds and Equal Error Rate (EER) analysis helped understand balanced screening performance. On the complete Tox21 test set, the 2D GIN achieved mean ROC-AUC of 0.9540, mean accuracy of 0.9059, mean F1-score of 0.8857, and average EER of 0.0893 across experiments, which is competitive with 3D methods while avoiding conformer generation overhead. This demonstrates that the proposed virtual node-augmented GIN framework maintains strong multi-task performance even across highly imbalanced toxicity datasets.

title: A Hybrid Deep Learning Framework for Multi-Class Lung Disease Classification
authors: Laxmi Bagalkot, Kelapati
publish: 2026 IEEE International Conference for Convergence in Computing Technology (I3CTCON)
abstract: Lung diseases remain a leading cause of morbidity and mortality worldwide, and timely, accurate diagnosis is critical for effective treatment. Although chest X-ray (CXR) imaging is widely used, manual interpretation is prone to human error, and existing machine learning and deep learning models often fail to simultaneously capture both local texture variations and global contextual features. Addressing this gap, this study proposes Multi-Stream Lung Disease Classifier (MSLDC), a novel framework designed to integrate gradient-based edge detection, multi-scale convolutional neural networks with channel-spatial attention, and a streamlined Vision Transformer for comprehensive multi-channel feature extraction. The objectives were to enhance sensitivity to subtle abnormalities, improve feature representation, and achieve robust multi-class classification across diverse lung disease categories. MSLDC was evaluated on two publicly available datasets, i.e., Chest X-Ray images dataset and IEEE data port dataset along with a combined dataset, achieving 99.95%, 99.95%, and 99.96% on the combined dataset, achieving better performance, minimal misclassifications, and high clinical reliability. These findings indicate that MSLDC effectively addresses current limitations and offers a scalable, accurate solution for automated lung disease detection.

title: Long-Term Meteorological Disaster Prediction for Low-Altitude Routes in Complex Micrometeorology Environments Using Deep Temporal Models
authors: Fei Sun
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: The rapid expansion of low-altitude operations for UAVs and eVTOL aircraft demands reliable long-horizon meteorological hazard prediction to support risk-aware route planning. Complex micrometeorology environments exhibit fast-changing and highly localized dynamics that are difficult to capture with conventional numerical weather prediction (NWP) at kilometerscale resolution, while local sensors provide high-fidelity observations but limited predictive capability beyond a few hours. This paper proposes MICNET, a deep temporal modeling framework that fuses high-frequency sensor observations with NWP forecast guidance for 24-72 hour aviation hazard forecasting along low-altitude routes. MICNET employs a transformer encoder-decoder backbone with a hierarchical spatial-temporal attention (H-STA) mechanism that explicitly decouples temporal evolution and spatial propagation, then adaptively fuses both through a learnable gate. We formulate a multi-task prediction problem over route waypoints, jointly forecasting wind shear probability, horizontal visibility, and turbulence intensity. Experiments on an 18-month dataset collected from three low-altitude routes in China demonstrate that MICNET consistently outperforms strong baselines. For 24-hour forecasts, MICNET improves windshear F1-score by 18.3% and reduces visibility RMSE by 23.7% and turbulence RMSE by 19.8%. The gains remain robust at 48-hour and 72-hour lead times, indicating MICNET's practical value for proactive safety management and long-term mission planning.

title: Lightweight Joint Optimization of Clustering and Trajectory for UAV-Assisted WSNs
authors: Chenhui Chu, Bo Xiao
publish: 2026 IEEE 8th International Conference on Communications, Information System and Computer Engineering (CISCE)
abstract: UAV-assisted data collection in wireless sensor networks offers flexibility and cost-efficiency, yet faces challenges from complex air-ground coupling and joint trajectory-resource optimization. Traditional deep reinforcement learning (DRL) methods still struggle with limited deployment capability on embedded platforms. This paper proposes a lightweight DQN framework with structured pruning to jointly optimize UAV path planning and cluster head selection. The approach employs forward-activation-based pruning for model compression. Simulations show that the proposed method outperforms ACO, with model size reduced by up to 90% while maintaining real-time performance suitable for UAV deployment.

title: Stochastic Analysis of Cramér-Rao Lower Bound for Positioning in mmWave-THz HetNets
authors: Jiajun He, Yiyong Sun, Feng Yin, Wenxin Xiong, Hing Cheung So, Hien Quoc Ngo, Hyundong Shin, Michail Matthaiou
abstract: Terahertz (THz) frequency band has been widely studied and is recognized as a promising candidate for centimeter-level localization. However, the limited coverage of THz networks may result in localization failures, while a heterogeneous deployment of millimeter-wave (mmWave) and THz radio units (RUs) offers a viable solution to mitigate this issue. This paper presents a theoretical framework for evaluating the performance limits of localization systems in mmWave and THz heterogeneous networks. In this architecture, the mmWave RUs serve as macro base stations (BSs), while the THz RUs function as micro BSs distributed around each mmWave RU. By leveraging the standard tools of stochastic geometry to model the spatial distributions of the RUs and ambient obstacles, the localizability of a target is computed to evaluate the probability of achieving sufficient signal-to-interference-plus-noise ratio for localization in both line-of-sight (LoS) and non-line-of-sight (NLoS) conditions. Furthermore, the Cramér-Rao lower bounds in both LoS and NLoS scenarios are analytically derived to characterize the overall positioning performance. Numerical results demonstrate that the hybrid deployment strategy significantly improves both the network coverage and localization accuracy compared to mmWave-only and THz-only networks.

title: Capacity-Optimized Design of Reconfigurable Antenna Patterns for MU-MIMO Systems
authors: Cuicui Zhang, Ming Zhang, Xiaoming Chen, Xiaobo Liu, Shitao Zhu, Anxue Zhang
abstract: Reconfigurable antennas have emerged as a promising solution in wireless communication systems to adapt to dynamic environments and enhance system performance. Pattern reconfigurability plays a crucial role in improving antenna adaptability to spatially diverse multi-cluster channels, thus maximizing channel capacity. Existing works primarily address either the mode selection problem from a pre-designed finite pattern set which adjusts only beamwidth and scanning direction and thus can not offer optimal channel adaptation, or the mode design problem of computing continuously adjustable patterns under the assumation of an infinite pattern number but without considering the limit on the pattern number in real-world antennas. To address this gap, this paper proposes a practical design methodology that jointly accounts for the finite number of reconfigurable patterns and their precise optimization over diverse channel conditions. Specifically, channels are grouped based on user location distribution, and a joint optimization approach combining alternating optimization, auxiliary variables, and bisection is employed to design the optimal pattern set under the constraint of limited reconfigurable states. Simulation results demonstrate the effectiveness of the optimized patterns and show that the achieved capacity exhibits an approximately logarithmic relationship with the number of reconfigurable patterns, thereby providing a practical validation of existing theoretical analyses. This work bridges the gap between theoretical mode design and practical implementation by offering a concrete and implementable solution for designing a finite set of reconfigurable antenna patterns tailored to realistic system constraints.

title: Next-Generation Solid-State Transformers for Powering Artificial Intelligence Data Centers: A Survey of Emerging Topologies
authors: Jonas Huber, Johann W. Kolar
abstract: The rapid growth of AI datacenters and the transition toward megawatt scale power consumption of single server racks demand a fundamental redesign of datacenter power distribution architectures. Conventional low voltage ac (LVac) systems face increasing limitations in efficiency, scalability, and material usage. Solid state transformers (SSTs) offer a pathway to compact medium voltage ac to low voltage dc (MVac LVdc) conversion, enabling MVac distribution directly to the server racks. However, today’s industrial fully modular SST demonstra-tors show limited power density of roughly 0.2 MW/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup>, which is insufficient for supporting the targeted high compute densities. This paper surveys emerging single-stage and quasi single-stage SST topologies with the potential for breaking through the power density barrier and achieving 1 MW/m<sup xmlns:mml="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML" xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink">3</sup> at 99% efficiency. Alternatives based on low-frequency transformers for near term adoption of LVdc distribution are also discussed. The paper highlights the need for a holistic comparative benchmarking of the different distribution architectures together with protection methods and SST concepts, especially including future MVdc distribution and MVdc-LVdc SSTs close to the server racks.
